AFFLICTION.BOUND: Most of this Affliction's logic lives in ChainsOfBindingPower.
AFFLICTION.ENTANGLED: This Affliction has no logic of its own. All logic lives in TangledPower.
AFFLICTION.GALVANIZED: Most of this Affliction's logic lives in GalvanicPower.
AFFLICTION.HEXED: Most of this Affliction's logic lives in HexPower.
AFFLICTION.RINGING: Most of this Affliction's logic lives in RingingPower.
AFFLICTION.SMOG: This Affliction has no logic of its own. All logic lives in SmoggyPower.
AFFLICTION.TAINTED: Most of this Affliction's logic lives in TaintedPower.
ENCHANTMENT.CLONE: This enchantment doesn't actually do anything on its own. Instead it is reference by cloneRestSiteOption when its looking for cards to duplicate.
ENUM.CardCostColor.Decreased: The current cost is modified to at/below normal. Colored green.
ENUM.CardCostColor.Increased: The current cost is modified to above normal. Colored blue.
ENUM.CardCostColor.InsufficientResources: The card cannot be played because the player does not have enough resources.
ENUM.CardEnergyCost._localModifiers: This card's local cost modifiers. See LocalCostModifier for details on how this works.
ENUM.CardKeywordOrder.afterDescription: All the keywords that should be added after the card's description. Order matters here. If a card has multiple keywords, they will appear in this order.
ENUM.CardKeywordOrder.beforeDescription: All the keywords that should be added before the card's description. Order matters here. If a card has multiple keywords, they will appear in this order.
ENUM.CardPreviewMode.MultiCreatureTarget: Calculate previews as if multiple creatures were being targeted at once. This is relevant for cards that target all/random enemies, where all those enemies may have the same power that would apply to the card. For example, if all enemies have VulnerablePower, attacks should preview the extra damage. If one enemy has it and another does not, attacks should not preview the extra damage. This is used in NCardPlay and similar areas.
ENUM.CardPreviewMode.None: Do not calculate any previews. This should be used on the backend when doing actual value calculations.
ENUM.CardPreviewMode.Normal: Calculate the "normal" previews (extra damage from StrengthPower, etc.) This should be the default when calculating a card's visuals.
ENUM.CardPreviewMode.Upgrade: Calculate previews as if the card were being upgraded. This is used in NUpgradePreview and similar areas.
ENUM.CardScope.Combat: A card that exists for a single combat. Cards in the player's draw/discard/exhaust/hand piles have the Combat scope, as do cards generated by effects like AttackPotion, shown in the upgrade preview for Armaments, etc.
ENUM.CardScope.None: A card that has no scope. Used in places like the Card Library. Should never be used during a run (even things like upgrade previews should have a scope).
ENUM.CardScope.Run: A card that exists for an entire run. Cards in the player's deck have the Run scope, as do cards offered at the merchant, during end-of-combat rewards, shown in the upgrade preview at the rest site, etc.
ENUM.CardTag.Defend: Makes this card a "Defend" card (DefendIronclad, DefendSilent, etc). Used by effects that apply to Defends, generally in tandem with a Basic rarity check (like PandorasBox), but also sometimes without (like Fasten).
ENUM.CardTag.Minion: Makes this card a "Minion" card (MinionDiveBomb, MinionSacrifice, etc). Used by effects that apply to Minion cards, like VitruvianMinion.
ENUM.CardTag.OstyAttack: Makes this card an "Osty Attack" card (Poke, Flatten, etc).
ENUM.CardTag.Strike: Makes this card a "Strike" card (StrikeIronclad, PommelStrike, etc). Used by effects that apply to Strikes, generally in tandem with a Basic rarity check (like PandorasBox), but also sometimes without (like PerfectedStrike and Hellraiser).
ENUM.CostModifiers.Global: Include any modifiers that are persistently applied by other models in the global game state, and that may change or wear off based on changes to other models in the game state.
ENUM.CostModifiers.Local: Include any modifiers that have been applied directly to the card. These modifiers live locally on the card itself, and will persist regardless of changes to other models in the combat state.
ENUM.CostModifiers.None: No modifiers at all. This will just return the card's unmodified energy cost. What is the difference between this and Canonical? A card's canonical cost is what would be "printed" on the card if it was printed out on paper, which means it cannot include _permanent_ modifications. The most prolific permanent modification is an upgrade; cards like Apotheosis reduce their cost by 1 when upgraded. This change will be reflected when using None, but not when calling Canonical.
ENUM.KeywordSources.Global: Include keywords that are persistently granted by other models in the combat state, and that wear off when that source changes or leaves. These are never stored on the card; they are computed on demand. HexPower grants Ethereal this way for as long as it exists.
ENUM.KeywordSources.Local: Include keywords that have been applied directly to the card. These live locally on the card itself (canonical keywords plus local CardKeyword) minus local CardKeyword)), and persist regardless of changes to other models in the combat state. MusicBox applies Ethereal this way.
ENUM.KeywordSources.None: No sources at all. This returns an empty set.
ENUM.LocalCostModifierExpiration.EndOfCo: This modifier should wear off when combat ends. Used for effects like Enlightenment and KinglyKick.
ENUM.LocalCostModifierExpiration.EndOfTu: This modifier should wear off when the turn ends. Used for effects like BulletTime and Pinpoint.
ENUM.LocalCostModifierExpiration.WhenPla: This modifier should wear off when the card is played. Used for effects like Eidolon.
ENUM.LocalCostType.Absolute: The card's local cost should be reset to this specific value.
ENUM.LocalCostType.Relative: The card's current local cost should be offset by this amount.
ENUM.PileType.Deck: Where cards live between rooms. When a new combat starts, all cards in here are cloned into your draw pile, so modifications to cards in combat won't modify cards in here.
ENUM.PileType.Hand: Where cards are manually played from.
ENUM.PileType.Play: A temporary pile that a card lives in while it's mid-play, so that it isn't counted towards your hand or your discard pile.
ENUM.TargetType.AllAllies: Targets all allies. No target selection.
ENUM.TargetType.AllEnemies: Targets all enemies. No target selection.
ENUM.TargetType.AnyAlly: Targets any ally (any player excluding itself). In multiplayer, target selection is performed to determine the player. You should not see this in singleplayer.
ENUM.TargetType.AnyEnemy: Can target any enemy. Target selection is performed to determine the enemy.
ENUM.TargetType.AnyPlayer: Targets any player. In multiplayer, target selection is performed to determine the player. In singleplayer, no target selection is performed.
ENUM.TargetType.RandomEnemy: Targets all enemies. No target selection.
ENUM.TargetType.Self: Can only target self. No target selection.
ENUM.TargetType.TargetedNoCreature: Target selection is performed, but no creature target is expected. Currently only used by FoulPotion to target the merchant, which is not a creature.
ENUM.UnplayableReason.BlockedByCardLogic: The card itself has a built-in reason that is blocking its play, like how Grand Finale can't be played unless your draw pile is empty.
ENUM.UnplayableReason.BlockedByHook: Something like Normality is blocking the card from being played.
ENUM.UnplayableReason.EnergyCostTooHigh: You don't have enough energy to play the card.
ENUM.UnplayableReason.HasUnplayableKeywo: The card has the Unplayable keyword, or its UnplayableThisTurn flag is set to true.
ENUM.UnplayableReason.NoLivingAllies: You have no living allies you can target.
ENUM.UnplayableReason.StarCostTooHigh: You don't have enough stars to play the card.
POWER.ACCELERANT_POWER: This power doesn't actually do anything on its own. Instead, the Poison power checks for this power and re-triggers itself if it's there.
POWER.BACK_ATTACK_LEFT_POWER: This is just a marker power for SurroundedPower to check for.
POWER.BACK_ATTACK_RIGHT_POWER: This is just a marker power for SurroundedPower to check for.
POWER.CLARITY_POWER: Draw an extra card at the beginning of your next N turns. This is distinct from DrawCardsNextTurnPower due to its stacking behavior.
POWER.DRAW_CARDS_NEXT_TURN_POWER: Draw an extra N cards at the beginning of your next turn. This is distinct from ClarityPower due to its stacking behavior.
POWER.ESCAPE_ARTIST_POWER: Just a visual timer for when ThievingHopper will escape.
POWER.FAN_OF_KNIVES_POWER: This power doesn't actually do anything on its own. Instead, the Shiv card checks for its existence and modifies its targeting.
POWER.HIBERNATE_POWER: This power doesn't actually do anything on its own. Instead, Frost orbs check for this power and change their behavior based off of that
POWER.PARRY_POWER: This power doesn't actually do anything on its own. Instead, the SovereignBlade card checks for its existence and modifies its block.
POWER.SEEKING_EDGE_POWER: This power doesn't actually do anything on its own. Instead, Sovereign blade cards checks for this power and and changes its behavior based off of that
POWER.TEMPORARY_DEXTERITY_POWER: This class represents a buff/debuff that gives/takes dexterity. We never instantiate this directly. See TemporaryStrengthPower for context around how this is used.
POWER.TEMPORARY_FOCUS_POWER: This class represents a buff/debuff that gives/takes focus. We never instantiate this directly. See TemporaryStrengthPower for context around how this is used.
POWER.TEMPORARY_STRENGTH_POWER: This class represents a buff/debuff that gives/takes strength. This class is never instantiated directly. Instead, it is subclassed and those classes represent the buff/debuff. Every model that grants a temporary strength power creates an associated power that subclasses this. For example, FlexPotion has FlexPotionPower which grants strength and then takes it away at the end of the turn. This used to be called Strength Down (or Restore Strength for negative strength) and be its own power. We switched to this model so make the UX more clear for players; both Strength and Temporary Strength are a buff.
POWER.THE_HUNT_POWER: This power acts as a visual indicator to the player that the Hunt card was successful. The actual behavior lives in TheHunt .
